title: RNA Isolation for Tissue
author: Chin Yee Tan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zvqf65w

steps.title: Homogenization in TriZol Add 1ml 1...
steps.instruction: Homogenization in TriZol Add 1ml 1 mL, Trizol per 30 mg, tissue and homogenize using handheld homogenizer.

steps.title: Incubate at RT for 00:05:00 to...
steps.instruction: Incubate at RT for 00:05:00 to allow nucleoprotein complexes to dissociate.

steps.title: Spin down at max speed in...
steps.instruction: Spin down at max speed in a chilled centrifuge for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Carefully remove the top aqueous phase...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the top aqueous phase and transfer to a new Eppendorf tube. The interphase and bottom organic phase can be saved for DNA and protein respectively.

steps.title: To the aqueous phase, add 500...
steps.instruction: To the aqueous phase, add 500 µL of 100% isopropanol, mix by inversion and incubate at -20 °C for a minimum for 02:00:00 .

steps.title: Spin down at max speed for 00:30:00 to precipitate RNA
steps.instruction: Spin down at max speed for 00:30:00 to precipitate RNA.

steps.title: Remove supernatant, and add 1 mL,...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant, and add 1 mL, 75% Ethanol to wash the pellet.

steps.title: Add 200 µL, Chloroform carefully, and vortex to mix well
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL, Chloroform carefully, and vortex to mix well.

steps.title: Spin down at max speed for 00:15:00 and remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Spin down at max speed for 00:15:00 and remove supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend pellet in appropriate volume of nuclease free water
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in appropriate volume of nuclease free water.
